facts.text: Sewell takes its name from U.S. Senator William Joyce Sewell, who helped shape the area in the 19th century.
The Jesse Chew House, built in 1772 from Jersey sandstone, sits on Mantua‑Sewell Road and is listed on the National Register of Historic Places.
Sewell hosts the Inversand marl pit, the world’s last operational greensand mine, a relic of the region’s mining past.
Paleontologists study fossils in Sewell, a site that echoes the early work of Edward Drinker Cope in the late 1800s.
Rowan College of South Jersey’s Sewell campus, opened in 2022, offers osteopathic medical training at 1474 Tanyard Road.
Perched on a bluff, Sewell overlooks Mantua Creek and lies about 16 miles southeast of Philadelphia, blending suburban life with natural scenery.
